channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19557] sanity test_56eaa: expected 'sanityusr' but got 'root' from file /mnt/lustre/d22.sanity/etc/hosts
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Chakshu Kansal <ckansal@ddn.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/c95b24a1-9af1-49ab-bb9a-de7cac072c1c

test_56eaa failed with the following error:

expected 'sanityusr' but got 'root' from file /mnt/lustre/d22.sanity/etc/hosts

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/118056 - 5.14.0-503.40.1.el9_5.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/118056 - 5.14.0-503.40.1_lustre.el9.x86_64

== sanity test 56eaa: test lfs find -printf added functions ========================================================== 13:12:29 (1761829949)
comparing 32315 elements starting with:
lfs: 144115188193296385 root root drwxrwxrwx /mnt/lustre
find: 144115188193296385 root root drwxrwxrwx /mnt/lustre
 sanity test_56eaa: @@@@@@ FAIL: expected 'sanityusr' but got 'root' from file /mnt/lustre/d22.sanity/etc/hosts>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity test_56eaa - expected 'sanityusr' but got 'root' from file /mnt/lustre/d22.sanity/etc/hosts
item.summary: sanity test_56eaa: expected 'sanityusr' but got 'root' from file /mnt/lustre/d22.sanity/etc/hosts

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Still occurred on master branch. 31 records were found within last 4 weeks.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62523/
Subject: LU-19557 llapi: fix race in printf username/group cache
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 5ea23e68b59d97f8752ef05ee35369eb7661b0dd

It is pretty clear that the race condition is as Patrick describes, even if it isn't clear whether regular automating can reproduce this on demand, since the failure rate is pretty low (10/ 1500=0.8% of sanity runs on master in the past week). Some of the test failures show partially-updated names like "saot", "roottyusr", "sani".

"Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62524
Subject: LU-19557 llapi: fix race in printf username/group cache
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e70a12fc71174178db6b5e9427dd1a3d7511524a

"Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62523
Subject: LU-19557 llapi: fix race in printf username/group cache
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 770d321a24820e89ad6a1b730afcdd922574c080

This is a regression failure introduced by master build #4660:
https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4660/changes
Among the above changes, it looks like the following commit is the culprit:

LU-17814 utils: Enable pfind by default

Add error handling and enable pfind by default.

This requires switching find_skip_file from counting to
probability based, otherwise we'd need an expensive atomic
op to coordinate the exact skip count.  But the point of the
option is statistical rather than exact behavior.

Increase the number of files in the relevant test to get
the law of large numbers in our favor.  Some complex math
suggests the probability of failure is extremely small.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I3dcbe94483873bd84d16dc02a860d70d314b9964
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57306

Hi paf0186, could you please take a look at this regression failure?

It looks like this was hit for the first time on 2025-10-23:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=f9516376-32bc-11e0-aaee-52540025f9ae&sub_test_script_id=c02c9e7b-ead1-4ef2-844d-b9be0aebfdc5&start_date=2025-09-01&end_date=2025-10-24&source=sub_tests#redirect

Looking at patches that landed on or slightly before that date:

$ git log --oneline --after 2025-10-20 --before 2025-10-24
f7753e70c7 LU-12738  test: improve write-append-truncate
c831d6d7e2 LU-19443 utils: show help for invalid interactive commands
1d6f4b47f4 LU-19422 quota: pool recalc must work even with one quota type
e3533028de LU-18506 osp: ensure args are freed
3759478588 LU-18667 kernel: new kernel [RHEL 10.0 6.12.0-55.37.1.el10_0]
be4e323b56 LU-18939 llite: allow readahead params to be set as percentage
1f3e4df2fb LU-18562 osd: llog write batch block allocation
a00b941661 LU-17814 utils: Enable pfind by default
c867a1260b LU-19462 lmv: send suppgids from client for cross-MDT ops
c2c3dc73dc LU-19323 tests: add lnet-selftest test smoke into IPv6 except list
1b3c10b835 LU-19386 lnet: use ip2nets handler for net sequence
f883eaa592 LU-19436 lnet: Correct ip2nets yaml parsing
bc3fe9e4bb LU-19435 osd-zfs: support for 2.4
99b251c971 LU-19424 lmv: expose qos_rr_index parameter
14cf3770b7 LU-19407 obdecho: Add kernel doc style for obdecho (1)
c564ff3825 LU-19355 quota: add option to print qid value
43ec76a4a9 LU-19296 utils: add llapi_convert_str2mask/mask2str()
36b493ef08 LU-19285 utils: Move yaml_parser_delete to yaml_parser_cleanup
97dae9b50e LU-19202 llite: revert truncate_inode_pages() under inode lock
ded36188d9 LU-16138 osd-ldiskfs: make BLK_DEV_INTEGRITY optional
5ca924c14a LU-13854 mdd: setattr can use multiple chglog types
